text: Three elements X, Y, Z are following Dobereiner's Triad rule. If the atomic weight of X and Y are 10 and 26 respectively, then atomic weight of Z will be
shortExplanation: According to Dobereiner's Triad rule, the atomic weight of the middle element (Y) is the arithmetic mean of the first (X) and third (Z) elements: 26 = (10 + Z)/2.

text: If Aufbau rule is not followed, K (Z = 19) will be placed in which block?
shortExplanation: If the Aufbau principle is ignored and shells are filled strictly by principal quantum number, the 19th electron would enter the 3d subshell instead of 4s, placing it in the d-block.

text: In the general electronic configuration (n-2)f¹⁻¹⁴ (n-1)d⁰⁻¹ ns², if value of n = 7 the configuration belongs to
shortExplanation: For n = 7, the electrons fill the 5f subshell, which characterizes the Actinide series.

text: Given atomic number 15, 33, 51 represents the following family:
shortExplanation: Atomic numbers 15 (P), 33 (As), and 51 (Sb) all belong to Group 15, which is the Nitrogen family.

text: Correct order of radius of iso-electronic species is:
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic species, the ionic radius decreases as the nuclear charge (atomic number) increases. Ca²⁺ (Z=20) is the smallest, and S²⁻ (Z=16) is the largest.

text: Which of the following has the maximum ionic radius?
shortExplanation: Ionic radius increases down a group in the periodic table due to the addition of new shells. Barium is the lowest in Group 2 among the options.

text: The correct order of the size of C, N, P, S is:
shortExplanation: Atomic size decreases across a period and increases down a group. N is smaller than C, and both are smaller than P and S. P is larger than S.

text: The set representing the correct order of first ionization potential is:
shortExplanation: Ionization potential decreases down a group. Be, Mg, and Ca are in Group 2, so Be has the highest and Ca the lowest IP.

text: The first ionization potential of Al is smaller than that of Mg because
options: Al has one electron in p-orbital
correctAnswer: Al has one electron in p-orbital
shortExplanation: It is easier to remove a 3p electron from Aluminum than a stable, paired 3s electron from Magnesium due to the subshell penetration effect.

text: Among the following, which element has the highest ionization energy?
shortExplanation: The configuration [Ne] 3s² 3p³ corresponds to Phosphorus, which has a half-filled p-subshell, providing extra stability and higher ionization energy.

text: Identify the correct order of electron affinity of following elements:
shortExplanation: Chlorine has a higher electron affinity than Fluorine because the small size of Fluorine leads to high inter-electronic repulsions in its 2p subshell.

text: In which of the following arrangements the order is NOT according to the property indicated against it?
options: Al³⁺ < Mg²⁺ < Na⁺ < F⁻ – increasing ionic size
B < C < N < O – increasing first ionization enthalpy
I < Br < F < Cl – increasing electron gain enthalpy
Li < Na < K < Rb – increasing metallic radius
correctAnswer: B < C < N < O – increasing first ionization enthalpy
shortExplanation: The first ionization enthalpy of Nitrogen is greater than Oxygen because Nitrogen has a stable half-filled 2p³ configuration.

text: Which of the following is most electronegative?
shortExplanation: Electronegativity generally decreases down a group. Carbon is at the top of Group 14.

text: The correct order of acidic strength of the following oxides is:
shortExplanation: Non-metal oxides (CO₂) are acidic, whereas metal oxides (CaO) are basic. Acidic character increases with the electronegativity of the central atom.

text: An element with atomic number 20 will be placed in which period of the periodic table?
shortExplanation: Calcium (Z=20) has the electronic configuration 2, 8, 8, 2. Since it has 4 shells, it belongs to the 4th period.

text: The element with electronic configuration 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁶ 4d¹⁰ 5s² 5p³ belongs to group
shortExplanation: The valence shell configuration is 5s² 5p³, which corresponds to 5 valence electrons, placing it in Group 15.

text: Eka-aluminium and Eka-silicon are known as
shortExplanation: Mendeleev predicted Gallium as Eka-aluminium and Germanium as Eka-silicon.

text: Which of the following is the correct order of the second ionization potential of C, N, O and F?
shortExplanation: After one electron is removed, O⁺ acquires a stable 2p³ configuration, making its second ionization energy exceptionally high.

text: Which of the following atoms has the highest first ionization energy?
shortExplanation: Scandium is a d-block element with a higher nuclear charge and smaller size compared to the alkali metals in the options.

text: The formation of the oxide ion O²⁻(g) requires an endothermic step as shown below: O⁻(g) + e⁻ → O²⁻(g); ΔH = +142 kJ/mol. This is because
options: O⁻ ion will resist the addition of another electron
correctAnswer: O⁻ ion will resist the addition of another electron
shortExplanation: Adding a second electron to a negatively charged ion (O⁻) involves strong electrostatic repulsion, requiring energy input.

text: Which of the following is the most basic oxide?
shortExplanation: Metallic character increases down a group. Bismuth is the most metallic element among the options, making its oxide the most basic.

text: The first ionization enthalpies of Na, Mg, Al and Si are in the order:
shortExplanation: Ionization energy generally increases across a period, but Mg is higher than Al due to its stable full 3s² subshell.

text: The most electronegative element of the period 3 is
shortExplanation: Electronegativity increases across a period. Chlorine is the rightmost non-noble gas element in Period 3.

text: Diagonal relationship is shown by:
shortExplanation: Diagonal relationship occurs between elements of the second and third periods that have similar charge/radius ratios.

text: Which of the following transition involves maximum energy?
shortExplanation: Successive ionization energies always increase because it becomes progressively harder to remove an electron from a more positively charged cation.

text: The electronic configuration of an element is 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p³. What is the atomic number of the element which is just below the above element in the periodic table?
shortExplanation: The given element is Phosphorus (Z=15). The element below it is Arsenic, which has an atomic number of 15 + 18 = 33.

text: The correct order of increasing radius is:
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic ions, the radius increases as the negative charge increases (decreasing nuclear charge per electron).

text: Screening effect is not observed in:
shortExplanation: Screening effect requires at least two electrons. Hydrogen has only one electron, so there are no inner electrons to screen it.

text: The period number in the long form of the periodic table is equal to:
options: Magnetic quantum number of any element of the period
Atomic number of any element of the period
Maximum Principal quantum number of any element of the period
Maximum Azimuthal quantum number of any element of the period
correctAnswer: Maximum Principal quantum number of any element of the period
shortExplanation: The period number corresponds to the highest principal quantum number (n) of the electrons in the elements of that period.

text: Elements with atomic number 38 belongs to:
shortExplanation: Atomic number 38 (Strontium) has the configuration [Kr] 5s². This places it in Group 2 (IIA) and Period 5.

text: The element with atomic number 117 has not been discovered yet. In which family would you place this element?
shortExplanation: Element 117 would follow the noble gas 118 (Oganesson) and belong to Group 17, which is the halogen family.

text: Which of the following is correct order of Zeff?
shortExplanation: Effective nuclear charge (Zeff) increases as electrons are removed (cation formation) and decreases as electrons are added (anion formation).

text: Variable valency is shown by:
shortExplanation: Transition elements show variable valency because the energy difference between (n-1)d and ns orbitals is very small, allowing both to participate in bonding.

text: Which is the smallest among the following?
shortExplanation: Atomic radius decreases across a period from left to right due to increasing effective nuclear charge. Silicon is the furthest right in this list.

text: The first ionization potential of Na, Mg, Al and Si are in the order:
shortExplanation: Mg has a higher first IP than Al due to its stable completely filled 3s² subshell.

text: The element with the highest electron affinity in the periodic table is:
shortExplanation: Chlorine has the highest electron affinity due to its optimal size which minimizes electron-electron repulsion compared to Fluorine.

text: Which of the following represents the correct order of increasing electron gain enthalpy with negative sign?
shortExplanation: Electron gain enthalpy follows the order O < S and F < Cl. Halogens have much higher values than group 16 elements.

text: The valency of an element with atomic number 9 is:
shortExplanation: Atomic number 9 is Fluorine (2, 7). It needs one electron to complete its octet, so its valency is 1.

text: Which of the following properties generally decreases along a period?
shortExplanation: Atomic radius decreases across a period as the nuclear charge increases while electrons are added to the same shell.

text: The IUPAC name of the element with atomic number 120 is:
shortExplanation: 1=un, 2=bi, 0=nil. Adding 'ium' gives Unbinilium.

text: The correct order of second ionization potential of Li, Be, B is:
shortExplanation: Li⁺ has a stable noble gas configuration (1s²), making its second ionization energy extremely high. B⁺ (1s² 2s²) is more stable than Be⁺ (1s² 2s¹).

text: Amphoteric oxide among the following is:
shortExplanation: ZnO can react with both acids and bases, making it an amphoteric oxide.

text: Representative elements belong to:
shortExplanation: Representative (or normal) elements include all s-block and p-block elements excluding noble gases.

text: The element having the lowest ionization enthalpy is:
shortExplanation: Cesium has the largest atomic size and lowest effective nuclear charge on its valence electron among the given choices.

text: Which of the following is not a transition element?
shortExplanation: Zinc (Zn) is not considered a transition element because it has a completely filled d-subshell (3d¹⁰) in its ground state and common oxidation state.

text: In the periodic table, the element with the highest electronegativity is found in:
shortExplanation: The most electronegative element is Fluorine, which is in Group 17, Period 2.

text: Which ion is isoelectronic with CO?
shortExplanation: CO has 6 + 8 = 14 electrons. CN⁻ has 6 + 7 + 1 = 14 electrons.

text: Which of the following follows Mendeleev's periodic law?
options: Properties are periodic functions of atomic numbers
Properties are periodic functions of atomic weights
Properties are periodic functions of electronic configurations
correctAnswer: Properties are periodic functions of atomic weights
shortExplanation: Mendeleev's periodic law states that the physical and chemical properties of elements are periodic functions of their atomic weights.

text: The element with atomic number 35 belongs to which block?
shortExplanation: Atomic number 35 is Bromine, which has the configuration [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁵, placing it in the p-block.

text: Which of the following gas is monoatomic?
shortExplanation: Noble gases like Helium have stable electronic configurations and exist as single atoms in the gas phase.

text: The correct order of increasing atomic radius of the following elements is:
shortExplanation: Atomic radius increases down a group and decreases across a period. Oxygen is the smallest, followed by Carbon, then Sulfur, and Selenium is the largest.

text: Choose correct order of ionization energies?
shortExplanation: Ionization energy is higher for smaller atoms and neutral atoms compared to their anions. F > Cl, and Cl- > F- due to electronic repulsions in the small F shell.

text: Which of the following order of radii is correct?
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic species and different ions of the same element, radius increases with increasing negative charge or decreasing positive charge.

text: The set representing the correct order for first ionisation potential is:
shortExplanation: Ionization potential decreases down the group. Be, Mg, and Ca belong to Group 2, so the order is Be > Mg > Ca.

text: In which of the following arrangements the order is NOT according to the property indicated against it?
options: Al3+ < Mg2+ < Na+ < F- (increasing ionic size)
B < C < N < O (increasing first ionisation enthalpy)
I < Br < F < Cl (increasing electron gain enthalpy with negative sign)
Li < Na < K < Rb (increasing metallic radius)
correctAnswer: B < C < N < O (increasing first ionisation enthalpy)
shortExplanation: The first ionisation enthalpy of Nitrogen is greater than Oxygen because Nitrogen has a stable half-filled p-subshell configuration.

text: Which of the following has the maximum density?
shortExplanation: Density generally increases down the group in alkali metals because the increase in atomic mass outweighs the increase in atomic volume.

text: The first ionisation potential of Al is smaller than that of Mg because
options: Al has one electron in p-orbital
The stopping power of s-orbital is less than p-orbital
correctAnswer: Al has one electron in p-orbital
shortExplanation: Mg has a fully filled 3s orbital which is more stable and closer to the nucleus than the single 3p electron in Al.

text: Which of the following is most electronegative?
shortExplanation: Electronegativity decreases down the group. Carbon, being the top element of group 14, is the most electronegative among these.

text: The correct order of acidic strength of the following oxides is:
shortExplanation: Basic character decreases and acidic character increases with increasing electronegativity of the element. CO2 is the most acidic non-metal oxide.

text: Which of the following elements has the highest ionization energy?
shortExplanation: The configuration [Ne] 3s2 3p3 corresponds to Phosphorus, which has a stable half-filled p-subshell.

text: The electronic configuration of an element is 1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p3. What is the atomic number of the element which is just below the above element in the periodic table?
shortExplanation: The element is Phosphorus (Z=15). The element below it is Arsenic, so 15 + 18 = 33.

text: Which of the following transition involves maximum energy?
shortExplanation: Successive ionization energies always increase. Removing an electron from a cation with a higher positive charge requires more energy.

text: The correct order of the second ionization potential of C, N, O and F is:
shortExplanation: Oxygen cation (O+) has a half-filled p-orbital (2p3), making its second ionization energy exceptionally high.

text: The formation of the oxide ion O2-(g) requires an endothermic step as shown below: O-(g) + e- → O2-(g); ΔH = +142 kJ/mol. This is because:
options: O- ion will resist the addition of another electron
correctAnswer: O- ion will resist the addition of another electron
shortExplanation: Adding an electron to a negative ion (O-) involves strong electrostatic repulsion, making the process endothermic.

text: Screening effect is not observed in:
shortExplanation: Screening effect is caused by inner shell electrons. Since Hydrogen has only one electron, there is no inner electron to screen the nucleus.

text: The ionic radii of N3-, O2- and F- are respectively:
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic species, the radius increases as the nuclear charge decreases (N < O < F atomic numbers). Thus N3- is largest and F- smallest.

text: Which of the following has the highest electron affinity?
shortExplanation: Chlorine has the highest electron affinity in the periodic table because the small size of Fluorine causes high inter-electronic repulsion.

text: The electronegativity of the following elements increases in the order:
shortExplanation: Electronegativity increases across a period and decreases down a group. Si and P are below C and N.

text: The process requiring absorption of energy is:
shortExplanation: The first electron gain enthalpy is usually exothermic, but adding the second electron (O- to O2-) is always endothermic due to repulsion.

text: In the long form of the periodic table, the element with atomic number 27 belongs to:
shortExplanation: Atomic number 27 is Cobalt ([Ar] 3d7 4s2), which is a transition metal located in the d-block.

text: The ionic radii of N³⁻, O²⁻ and F⁻ are respectively:
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic species, the radius increases as the negative charge increases. Nitrogen has the smallest atomic number (7) among these, so N³⁻ has the least nuclear pull per electron, making it the largest.

text: Which of the following has the highest electron affinity?
shortExplanation: While Fluorine is more electronegative, Chlorine has the highest electron affinity because its larger 3p subshell can accommodate an extra electron with less inter-electronic repulsion than Fluorine's 2p subshell.

text: The electronegativity of the following elements increases in the order:
shortExplanation: Electronegativity increases up a group and to the right across a period. Si and P (Period 3) are lower than C and N (Period 2).

text: The process requiring absorption of energy is:
shortExplanation: The second electron gain enthalpy (forming O²⁻ from O⁻) is always endothermic because the incoming electron is repelled by the already negative ion.

text: Which of the following elements will have the highest second ionization enthalpy?
shortExplanation: Sodium (Na) becomes Na⁺ after the first ionization, which has a stable noble gas configuration (2s² 2p⁶). Removing a second electron requires breaking this stable octet.

text: The correct order of first ionisation enthalpy is:
shortExplanation: Mg > Al due to stable 3s² configuration; P > S due to stable half-filled 3p³ configuration.

text: The element with the outermost electronic configuration 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p³ belongs to:
shortExplanation: The group number for p-block elements is 10 + number of valence electrons (2+3 = 5), so it belongs to Group 15.

text: Which of the following represents the correct order of metallic character?
shortExplanation: Metallic character increases down a group and decreases across a period. Na is the most metallic, followed by Mg, Be, and then the metalloid Si.

text: The IUPAC nomenclature of an element with electronic configuration [Rn] 5f¹⁴ 6d¹ 7s² is:
shortExplanation: Atomic number = 86 (Rn) + 14 + 1 + 2 = 103. 1 = un, 0 = nil, 3 = tri. Name: Unniltrium.

text: The first ionization energy of magnesium is smaller than elements X and Y, but higher than Z. X, Y and Z respectively are:
shortExplanation: Argon and Chlorine (to the right of Mg) have higher IE, while Sodium (to the left of Mg) has lower IE.

text: Which of the following atoms has the highest first ionization energy?
shortExplanation: Scandium (Sc) is a d-block element with a significantly higher nuclear charge and smaller atomic radius compared to the alkali metals listed, leading to a stronger attraction for the valence electron.

text: The element with atomic number 117 has not been discovered yet. In which family would you place this element?
shortExplanation: The noble gas before it is element 118 (Group 18). Therefore, element 117 would be one position to the left, placing it in Group 17, the Halogen family.

text: Which of the following transition elements has the lowest enthalpy of atomisation?
shortExplanation: Enthalpy of atomisation depends on the number of unpaired electrons available for metallic bonding. Zinc (Zn) has a completely filled d-subshell (3d¹⁰ 4s²), resulting in very weak metallic bonds.

text: The correct order of increasing radius is:
shortExplanation: These are isoelectronic species. As the negative charge increases (and nuclear charge decreases), the electron cloud expands, so As³⁻ is the largest and Br⁻ is the smallest.

text: The correct order of second ionization potential of Li, Be, and B is:
shortExplanation: Li⁺ has a stable noble gas configuration (1s²), making its second IE extremely high. B⁺ (2s²) is more stable than Be⁺ (2s¹), thus IE₂ of B > Be.

text: Which of the following represents the correct order of electron gain enthalpy (with negative sign)?
shortExplanation: Group 17 has higher electron gain enthalpy than Group 16. Within each group, the second element (Cl, S) has a higher value than the first (F, O) due to less electronic repulsion in larger orbitals.

text: Which of the following is an amphoteric oxide?
shortExplanation: Aluminum oxide (Al₂O₃) reacts with both strong acids and strong bases to form salts and water, demonstrating amphoteric behavior.

text: The element having the lowest ionization enthalpy in the periodic table is:
shortExplanation: Ionization enthalpy decreases down a group. Cesium (Cs) is generally cited as having the lowest stable IE, as Francium (Fr) is radioactive and its properties are less conventional.

text: The valency of an element with atomic number 33 is:
shortExplanation: Atomic number 33 is Arsenic (Group 15). It has 5 valence electrons and can show valencies of 3 (by sharing/gaining 3) or 5 (by involving d-orbitals).

text: The element with the highest density in the 3d transition series is:
shortExplanation: In the 3d series, density increases from Sc to Cu because the atomic mass increases significantly while the atomic radius decreases or remains relatively small.

text: Which of the following is the correct order of ionic radii?
shortExplanation: For the same element, the radius of a cation decreases as the oxidation state (positive charge) increases due to higher effective nuclear charge.

text: Diagonal relationship is shown by:
shortExplanation: Diagonal relationship exists between certain elements of the 2nd and 3rd periods (Li-Mg, Be-Al, B-Si) due to similar ionic sizes and electronegativities.

text: The most basic oxide among the following is:
shortExplanation: Basic character of oxides increases as the metallic character of the element increases. Sodium (Na) is the most metallic element in this set.

text: Which of the following factors does not affect the periodic trends of elements?
shortExplanation: Modern periodic trends are determined by electronic configuration and effective nuclear charge, not by atomic mass.

text: The number of p-block elements among the following atomic numbers 3, 11, 21, 31 is:
shortExplanation: 3 (Li) and 11 (Na) are s-block. 21 (Sc) is d-block. Only 31 (Ga) is a p-block element.

text: The ionization energy of magnesium is smaller as compared to that of elements X and Y, but higher than that of Z. The elements X, Y and Z respectively, are:
shortExplanation: Argon and Chlorine (to the right of Mg) have higher IE. Sodium (to the left of Mg) has lower IE.

text: The outermost electronic configuration of the most electronegative element is:
shortExplanation: The Halogens (Group 17) are the most electronegative elements, characterized by the ns²np⁵ valence configuration.

text: The group number, number of valence electrons and valency of an element with atomic number 15, respectively, are:
shortExplanation: Atomic number 15 (Phosphorus) has the configuration 2, 8, 5. It is in Group 15, has 5 valence electrons, and a typical valency of 3.

text: The correct order of first ionisation enthalpy is:
shortExplanation: Mg (3s²) > Al (3p¹) due to subshell stability. P (3p³) > S (3p⁴) due to half-filled subshell stability.

text: The IUPAC nomenclature of an element with electronic configuration [Rn] 5f¹⁴ 6d¹ 7s² is:
shortExplanation: The atomic number is 86 (Rn) + 14 + 1 + 2 = 103. According to IUPAC rules: 1 (un), 0 (nil), 3 (tri) + ium = Unniltrium.

text: If IUPAC name of an element is 'Unununnium' then the element belongs to nth group of periodic table. The value of n is [30 Jan, 2024 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: Unununnium has atomic number 111. Elements with atomic numbers 111 belong to Group 11 (d-block). [cite: 53, 54]

text: It is observed that characteristic X-ray spectra of elements show regularity. When frequency to the power 'n' i.e. v^n of X-rays emitted is plotted against atomic number 'Z', a linear graph is obtained. The value of 'n' is [24 Jan, 2023 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: According to Moseley's Law, √v = a(Z - b). Therefore, the frequency raised to the power 1/2 is proportional to the atomic number Z. [cite: 54, 55]

text: Henry Moseley studied characteristic X-ray spectra of elements. The graph which represents his observation correctly is: (Given v frequency of X-ray emitted; Z = atomic number) [8 April, 2023 (Shift-II)]
options: Graph of log v vs Z
shortExplanation: Moseley's observation showed a linear relationship between the square root of frequency (√v) and the atomic number (Z). [cite: 55]

text: The IUPAC nomenclature of an element with electronic configuration [Rn] 5f¹⁴ 6d¹ 7s² is: [25 July, 2022 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: The atomic number is 103 (Rn=86 + 14 + 1 + 2). Roots for 1, 0, 3 are un, nil, tri. [cite: 55, 56]

text: Which one of the following statements for D.I. Mendeleev's, is incorrect? [22 July, 2021 (Shift-II)]
options: At the time, he proposed periodic table of elements structure of atom was known
Element with atomic number 101 is named after him
He authored the textbook - Principles of Chemistry
correctAnswer: At the time, he proposed periodic table of elements structure of atom was known
shortExplanation: Mendeleev proposed the periodic table before the internal structure of the atom (electrons, protons, neutrons) was known. [cite: 56, 63]

text: The atomic number of Unnilunium is [6 Sept, 2020 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Un (1), Nil (0), Un (1) corresponds to atomic number 101. [cite: 56]

text: The atomic number of the element unnilennium is [03 Sept, 2020 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: Un (1), Nil (0), Enn (9) corresponds to atomic number 109. [cite: 56]

text: The IUPAC symbol for the element with atomic number 119 would be: [18 April, 2019 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Atomic number 119: Un (u), Un (u), Enn (e). Symbol is uue. [cite: 56]

text: Statement-I: The 4f and 5f-series of elements are placed separately in the Periodic table to preserve the principle of classification. Statement-II: S-block elements can be found in pure form in nature. [27 Jan, 2024 (Shift-I)]
options: Statement-I is false but Statement-II is true
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are true
Statement-I is true but Statement-II is false
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are false
correctAnswer: Statement-I is true but Statement-II is false
shortExplanation: Statement I is true; Statement II is false as s-block elements are highly reactive and found as compounds. [cite: 57, 58, 59]

text: Statement-I: Both metal and non-metal exist in p- and d-block elements. Statement-II: Non-metals have higher ionisation enthalpy and higher electronegativity than the metals. [01 Feb, 2024 (Shift-II)]
options: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are false
Statement-I is false but Statement-II is true
Statement-I is true but Statement-II is false
Both Statement-I and Statement-II are true
correctAnswer: Statement-I is false but Statement-II is true
shortExplanation: d-block consists only of metals. p-block contains metals, non-metals, and metalloids. [cite: 59, 60, 61]

text: Match List-I with List-II: A. N (I. [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁵), B. S (II. [Ne] 3s² 3p⁴), C. Br (III. [He] 2s² 2p³), D. Kr (IV. [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁶) [09 April, 2024 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Nitrogen is [He] 2s² 2p³; Sulfur is [Ne] 3s² 3p⁴; Bromine is [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁵; Krypton is [Ar] 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁶. [cite: 61, 63]

text: The number of s-electrons present in an ion with 55 protons in its unipositive state is [24 Jan, 2023 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Cs (Z=55) loses 1e⁻ to become Cs⁺. Configuration: 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p⁶ 3d¹⁰ 4s² 4p⁶ 4d¹⁰ 5s² 5p⁶. Total s-electrons = 2+2+2+2+2 = 10. [cite: 63]

text: Match Atomic number with Block: A. 37 (I. p-block), B. 78 (II. d-block), C. 52 (III. f-block), D. 65 (IV. s-block) [30 Jan, 2023 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: 37 is Rubidium (s-block); 78 is Platinum (d-block); 52 is Tellurium (p-block); 65 is Terbium (f-block). [cite: 65]

text: The total number of isoelectronic species from the given set is O²⁻, F⁻, Al, Mg²⁺, Na⁺, O⁺, Mg, Al³⁺, F [15 April, 2023 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: O²⁻, F⁻, Mg²⁺, Na⁺, and Al³⁺ all have 10 electrons. [cite: 65]

text: Which one of the following sets of ions represents a collection of isoelectronic species? (Atomic Number: F: 9, Cl: 17, Na: 11, Mg: 12, Al: 13, K: 19, Ca: 20, Sc: 21) [1 Feb, 2023 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: K⁺, Ca²⁺, Sc³⁺, and Cl⁻ all have 18 electrons. [cite: 65, 66]

text: The correct order of ionic radii for the ions, P³⁻, S²⁻, Ca²⁺, K⁺, Cl⁻ is: [26 June, 2022 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic species, the ionic radius decreases as the nuclear charge (atomic number) increases. Atomic numbers: P(15), S(16), Cl(17), K(19), Ca(20).

text: The ionic radii (in Å) of Sb³⁺ and Te²⁻ are respectively: [29 June, 2022 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Anions are always larger than their parent atoms and corresponding cations. Te²⁻ (anion) will have a much larger radius than Sb³⁺ (cation).

text: The first ionization enthalpy of Na, Mg and Si are 496, 737 and 786 kJ/mol respectively. The first ionization enthalpy of Al will be closer to: [26 Aug, 2021 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Ionization enthalpy of Al is less than Mg because it is easier to remove a 3p¹ electron than a stable 3s² electron due to lower penetration power and effective shielding.

text: The electronic configuration of an element is 1s² 2s² 2p⁶ 3s² 3p³. The atomic number of the element present just below this element in the same group of the periodic table is: [24 Feb, 2021 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: The configuration is for Phosphorus (Z=15). The element below it in the same group (Group 15) is Arsenic, which has an atomic number of 15 + 18 = 33.

text: The set that contains atomic numbers of only transition elements is: [17 March, 2021 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: Transition elements belong to the d-block (Groups 3-12). Atomic numbers 21(Sc), 25(Mn), 42(Mo), and 72(Hf) are all transition metals.

text: The first ionization energy of magnesium is smaller than as compared to that of elements X and Y, but higher than that of Z. The elements X, Y and Z, respectively, are: [25 Feb, 2021 (Shift-II)]
shortExplanation: Mg has higher IE than alkali metals like Na (Z), but lower than non-metals/noble gases like Cl and Ar (X, Y) located to its right in the period.

text: Which of the following elements will have the highest second ionization enthalpy? [JEE Main 2020]
shortExplanation: After losing one electron, Na⁺ attains a stable noble gas configuration (2s² 2p⁶). Removing a second electron from this stable octet requires very high energy.

text: The correct order of the atomic radii of C, Cs, Al and S is: [11 Jan, 2019 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: Atomic radius decreases across a period (C > S) and increases down a group (Cs is in Period 6, making it the largest). C is the smallest.

text: The IUPAC name of the element with atomic number 120 is: [JEE Main 2019]
shortExplanation: For Z=120: 1 (un), 2 (bi), 0 (nil) + 'ium' = Unbinilium.

text: In general, the properties that decrease and increase down a group in the periodic table, respectively, are: [9 Jan, 2019 (Shift-I)]
shortExplanation: Down a group, atomic radius increases due to addition of shells, while electronegativity decreases due to increased shielding and distance from the nucleus.

text: The correct order of electron gain enthalpy with negative sign is: [JEE Main 2018]
shortExplanation: Chlorine has the highest negative electron gain enthalpy. Fluorine is slightly less due to inter-electronic repulsions in its small 2p orbital.

text: Which of the following atoms has the highest first ionization energy? [JEE Main 2017]
shortExplanation: Ionization energy generally increases from left to right across a transition series due to increase in effective nuclear charge.

text: The ionic radii of N³⁻, O²⁻ and F⁻ are respectively: [JEE Main 2015]
shortExplanation: For isoelectronic species, as the magnitude of negative charge increases, the radius increases. N³⁻ (largest) > O²⁻ > F⁻ (smallest).

text: Which of the following represents the correct order of increasing first ionization enthalpy for Ca, Ba, S, Se and Ar? [JEE Main 2013]
shortExplanation: Noble gases (Ar) have the highest IE. IE decreases down a group (Ba < Ca and Se < S). S and Se are to the right of Ca and Ba, so they have higher IE.

text: The first ionization potential of Na, Mg, Al and Si are in the order: [JEE Main 2012]
shortExplanation: IE increases across a period, but Mg (3s²) is higher than Al (3p¹) because removing an electron from a fully filled subshell is harder.

text: The correct order of electron gain enthalpy with negative sign for F, Cl, Br and I is: [2011]
shortExplanation: Cl has the highest negative electron gain enthalpy in the periodic table, followed by F, then Br and I.

text: Which one of the following is an amphoteric oxide? [2010]
shortExplanation: ZnO can react with both acids and bases to form salts, identifying it as an amphoteric oxide.

text: The outer electron configuration of Gd (Atomic No. 64) is: [2011]
shortExplanation: Gadolinium has an exceptional configuration where one electron enters the 5d orbital to maintain a stable half-filled 4f⁷ subshell.

text: The increasing order of the ionic radii of the given isoelectronic species is: [2008]
shortExplanation: Radii order for isoelectronic: Cation with highest charge is smallest, Anion with highest charge is largest. Ca²⁺ < K⁺ < Cl⁻ < S²⁻.

text: Which of the following has the highest first ionization energy? [2007]
shortExplanation: Across a period (Li to C), the ionization energy increases with increasing nuclear charge. Carbon has the highest in this group.

text: The correct order of increasing oxidizing power of the following is: [2006]
shortExplanation: Oxidizing power is directly related to electron gain enthalpy and electronegativity, which decrease down the halogen group.

text: Which of the following is the most electronegative element? [2005]
shortExplanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element in the entire periodic table due to its small size and high effective nuclear charge.

text: Which of the following ions has the smallest radius? [2004]
shortExplanation: B³⁺ has the highest positive charge among the cations in the second period, resulting in the strongest nuclear pull and smallest radius.

text: The element with atomic number 117 would belong to: [2003]
shortExplanation: Element 117 is one position before the noble gas 118, placing it in Group 17, the Halogen family.

text: According to the periodic law of elements, the variation in properties of elements is related to their: [2002]
shortExplanation: The Modern Periodic Law states that properties are a periodic function of atomic numbers.

text: Which of the following is the correct order of radius? [2001]
shortExplanation: Anions are larger than neutral atoms, and cations are smaller than neutral atoms.

text: The first ionization potential of nitrogen and oxygen atoms are related as: [2000]
options: IE of N > IE of O
IE of O > IE of N
IE of N = IE of O
correctAnswer: IE of N > IE of O
shortExplanation: Nitrogen has a stable half-filled 2p³ configuration, making its ionization energy higher than Oxygen (2p⁴).

text: Which of the following sets of elements represents only metalloids? [1999]
shortExplanation: Silicon, Germanium, and Arsenic are well-known metalloids located along the zig-zag line in the p-block.

text: The diagonal relationship of Li is with: [1998]
shortExplanation: Lithium shows a diagonal relationship with Magnesium due to their similar charge/radius ratios.

text: The screening effect of d-electrons is: [1997]
shortExplanation: The shielding power order is s > p > d > f. Thus, d-electrons shield less effectively than p-electrons.

text: Which of the following has the maximum density? [1996]
shortExplanation: Osmium (Os) is one of the densest naturally occurring elements.

text: The electronegativity of elements C, N, Si and P increases in the order: [1995]
shortExplanation: Electronegativity increases across a period and decreases down a group. Si and P are below C and N, respectively.

text: The electronic configuration of a transition element X in +3 oxidation state is [Ar]3d⁵. Its atomic number is: [1994]
shortExplanation: X³⁺ = [Ar]3d⁵. Adding 3 electrons back: [Ar]3d⁶ 4s². Z = 18 + 6 + 2 = 26 (Iron).

text: In the Mendeleev periodic table, silver belongs to group: [1993]
shortExplanation: In the old Mendeleev system, coinage metals (Cu, Ag, Au) were placed in Group IB.

text: The element with the maximum first ionization energy is: [1992]
shortExplanation: Helium has the highest first ionization energy of all elements due to its stable 1s² configuration and very small radius.
